children.heading: Bona fide purchaser
children.label: 1 Bona fide purchaser
children.text: The term “bona fide purchaser” means a purchaser for value in good faith and without notice of any adverse claim, and who acquires the security property free of any adverse claim.

children.heading: County
children.label: 2 County
children.text: The term “county” has the same meaning as in .section 2 of title 1

children.heading: Mortgage
children.label: 3 Mortgage
children.text: The term “mortgage” means a deed of trust, mortgage, deed to secure debt, security agreement, or any other form of instrument under which any property (real, personal or mixed), or any interest in property (including leaseholds, life estates, reversionary interests, and any other estates under applicable State law), is conveyed in trust, mortgaged, encumbered, pledged, or otherwise rendered subject to a lien for the purpose of securing the payment of money or the performance of an obligation.

children.heading: Mortgage agreement
children.label: 4 Mortgage agreement
children.text: The term “mortgage agreement” means the note or debt instrument and the mortgage instrument, deed of trust instrument, trust deed, or instrument or instruments creating the mortgage, including any instrument incorporated by reference therein and any instrument or agreement amending or modifying any of the foregoing.

children.heading: Mortgagor
children.label: 5 Mortgagor
children.text: The term “mortgagor” means the obligor, grantor, or trustee named in the mortgage agreement and, unless the context otherwise indicates, includes the current owner of record of the security property whether or not such owner is personally liable on the mortgage debt.

children.heading: Owner
children.label: 6 Owner
children.text: The term “owner” means any person who has an ownership interest in property and includes heirs, devises, executors, administrators, and other personal representatives, and trustees of testamentary trusts if the owner of record is deceased.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 7 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes any individual, group of individuals, association, partnership, corporation, or organization.

children.heading: Record; recorded
children.label: 8 Record; recorded
children.text: The terms “record” and “recorded” include “register” and “registered” in the instance of registered land.

children.heading: Security property
children.label: 9 Security property
children.text: The term “security property” means the property (real, personal or mixed) or an interest in property (including leaseholds, life estates, reversionary interests, and any other estates under applicable State law), together with fixtures and other interests subject to the lien of the mortgage under applicable State law.
